summary: Validated the two new Embla carousels on THE VIEW luxury site. Amenities carousel on /the-view works flawlessly. Gallery editorial fade carousel on /gallery renders and functions overall, but the prev/next arrow buttons exhibit flaky behavior (occasional lost first click / skipped slide) — dots (scrollTo) work reliably. Lightbox, masonry filters, walkthrough regression and backend smoke are all clean.

ui_bugs.issue: Prev/Next arrow clicks are unreliable — first click after page load is frequently swallowed (counter stays on 01/06), and subsequent rapid clicks sometimes skip a slide (e.g. 02 → 04 or 05 → 01 skipping 06). Reproducible with 1.5s AND 3s waits between clicks even with the carousel hovered (autoplay paused). Dot buttons (gallery-hero-dot-N via scrollTo) always work correctly. Suspected cause: race between Autoplay's initial 5.5s timer / stopOnInteraction handshake and manual scrollNext/scrollPrev calls while a fade transition is in flight. Consider (a) calling emblaApi.plugins().autoplay?.stop() inside scrollPrev/scrollNext callbacks before scrolling, or (b) increasing duration guard, or (c) using stopOnInteraction:false + stopOnMouseEnter:true and gating advances until emblaApi is idle.

issues: Because the Fade plugin stacks slides on top of each other, the two absolute-positioned bottom-left captions (category + title) of the outgoing and incoming slides briefly overlap and become illegible mid-fade (e.g. 'Cubhouse Lounge/Study' garbled). Consider fading the caption independently or hiding non-selected captions via opacity.

action_items: Investigate and fix flaky gallery hero prev/next behavior — see suggested fixes above. Repro: open /gallery, hover the hero to pause autoplay, click next; counter often stays at 01/06 for the first click.
Optional polish: fade slide captions independently so mid-transition overlap doesn't create garbled text.

Doc/testid alignment: The review request references data-testid='the-view-amenities' but the actual attribute in code is 'theview-amenities' (from constants/testIds/site.js THE_VIEW_PAGE.amenities). Either update the constant or update docs — testers will hit this mismatch.
critical_code_review_comments: GalleryEditorialCarousel uses className with escaped brackets ('.max-h-[85vh]') which is fine for Tailwind, but the counter and 'Click to enlarge' both share .font-ui class without a data-testid on the counter. Adding data-testid='gallery-hero-counter' on the top-right counter div would make it robust for tests.

Both new carousels correctly register embla event listeners in useEffect but only clean up 'select' — the 'reInit' handler in AmenitiesCarousel is never removed (minor memory / handler-leak on unmount). Suggest storing the reInit handler and calling emblaApi.off('reInit', handler) in cleanup.
AmenitiesCarousel imports AMENITIES from siteData; the review says images are added per amenity — confirmed 8 images load. Consider adding width/height attributes to for CLS.
Gallery lightbox click uses e.stopPropagation() correctly so backdrop-close works, but the close X button lacks a data-testid — add data-testid='gallery-lightbox-close' for future tests.
seed_data_creation: One TEST_smoke lead was created via /api/leads for backend smoke check.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Amenities carousel on /the-view (data-testid 'theview-amenities' — NOT 'the-view-amenities') is fully validated with all 8 slides, 8 dots, arrows, dot navigation and hover-pause working. Gallery hero (/gallery, data-testid 'gallery-editorial-carousel') renders with 6 slides, dots work, lightbox opens/closes, but arrow prev/next is flaky. After main agent's fix, focus retest on: click next 6 times in a row (with 2s waits and hover to pause autoplay), verify counter goes 01→02→03→04→05→06→01 monotonically. Then click prev 6 times and verify reverse. Walkthrough & backend (/api/leads, /api/chat) are green.
rca of the issue: Gallery hero flaky arrows RCA: Autoplay 5.5s + Fade 30ms duration + stopOnInteraction=true. When component mounts, Autoplay schedules its first tick at t=5500ms. During those 5.5s, if the user clicks next, embla acquires the pointer interaction — Autoplay stops — but the click's scrollNext() call may collide with an in-flight internal 'select' event or be re-entered by embla's own click-during-scroll guard, resulting in the click being consumed without advancement. Subsequent rapid clicks then either double-advance (queued) or are ignored. Reproduction: /gallery, wait ~3s, click gallery-hero-next once, wait 3s — counter stays 01/06 in ~50% of runs. Mitigation: explicitly stop autoplay before manual scroll (emblaApi.plugins().autoplay?.stop()) inside scrollPrev/scrollNext callbacks, or bump Fade duration to 500-800ms so single-shot fades resolve cleanly before next click.
